anchor: Adrenal androgen conversion contributes less than 5 percent of total testosterone production in males with normal gonadal function.
caveat: Not a daily mass production value.

anchor: About 5-10 ug/day testis and 40-45 ug/day peripheral tissues.
source_locator: Diagnostic Aspects: Significance of Serum Estradiol in Men
caveat: Approximate source split; cross-check with spermatogenesis chapter.

anchor: About 20 percent of circulating DHT in men is secreted by testes; remainder is from peripheral testosterone bioconversion.
caveat: Circulating DHT source statement, not tissue DHT activity.

anchor: DHEAS concentrations are about 300 times free DHEA; DHEAS has no diurnal rhythm while DHEA peaks in the morning.
caveat: Timing note, not a production-rate row.

anchor: Human gonadal sampling and BSO data did not support gonads as material sources of circulating 11-oxygenated androgens.
caveat: Source-split context only; not a population reference interval.

anchor: Davio 2020 reported sustained 11OHA4 and 11OHT with aging and male-only declines in 11KT and 11KA4.
caveat: Adult trend context only; do not treat as an extracted reference interval.

anchor: Fit Futures reported lower mean 11KT, 11OHT, and 11KA4 in combined hormonal contraceptive users than in non-CHC users.
caveat: Mean comparison only; interval rows remain source-specific Table 3 bands.

anchor: Liu 2026 measures plasma 5-alpha-DHP, 17OH-DHP, 17OH-Allo, androsterone, unconjugated 3-alpha-diol, and DHT as androgen backdoor-pathway steroids.
caveat: Backdoor-pathway context only; do not merge unconjugated 3-alpha-diol with 3-alpha-diol glucuronide.
